libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image, external WebDAV server, repository, or environment/system property is required. For servlet-facing coverage, use small in-memory HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse stubs and local DavSessionProvider/DavLocatorFactory/DavResourceFactory test doubles rather than starting a servlet container. Use legacy javax.servlet APIs, not jakarta.servlet.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The jackrabbit-webdav POM declares javax.servlet:servlet-api with provided scope, and public classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, InputContextImpl, OutputContextImpl, and several header parsers reference javax.servlet types. ...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Without the provided Servlet API on the test classpath, tests that touch the server-side WebDAV APIs will fail to compile or load classes.
The library documentation states this artifact is a common server/client library, not a full deployable Jackrabbit WebDAV server, so tests should avoid requiring a running repository or webapp.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jackrabbit WebDAV can be tested without external services, but meaningful coverage of its server/request/response/header/io APIs needs the legacy javax Servlet API because the artifact declares it as a provided dependency rather than a transitive runtime dependency.
